tools.name: query
tools.description: Execute a SQL query against the database. Returns JSON results with column names, rows, and cost breakdown.

tools.name: get_schema
tools.description: Get the full database schema including tables, columns, types, indexes, foreign keys, and PII classifications.

tools.name: get_table
tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific table including columns, indexes, foreign keys, and sample values.

tools.name: get_relationships
tools.description: Get the relationship map showing how tables are connected via foreign keys.

tools.name: search_columns
tools.description: Search for columns by name or type across all tables.

tools.name: list_pii
tools.description: List all columns classified as containing personally identifiable information (PII).

tools.name: create_sandbox
tools.description: Create an isolated sandbox environment (instant branch) for safe experimentation. Returns a scoped token for the sandbox.

tools.name: destroy_sandbox
tools.description: Destroy a sandbox environment and revoke its token.

tools.name: optimize_query
tools.description: Analyze a SQL query and return optimization suggestions including index recommendations and query rewrites.

tools.name: nl_to_sql
tools.description: Translate a natural language question to SQL using the database schema context.

tools.name: scalix_db_query
tools.description: Execute a SQL query against the project database. Returns columns, rows, row count, and cost breakdown.

tools.name: scalix_db_schema
tools.description: Get the full database schema including all tables, columns, types, and relationships.

tools.name: scalix_db_table
tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific database table including columns, indexes, and foreign keys.

tools.name: scalix_db_sandbox
tools.description: Create an isolated database sandbox (instant branch) for safe experimentation. Returns a scoped token.

tools.name: scalix_db_migrate
tools.description: Apply a database migration by version number.

tools.name: scalix_storage_list
tools.description: List storage buckets, or list objects in a specific bucket with optional prefix filter.

tools.name: scalix_storage_upload
tools.description: Upload content to object storage. Provide the content as a base64-encoded string.

tools.name: scalix_storage_download
tools.description: Download an object from storage. Returns the content as base64.

tools.name: scalix_storage_create_bucket
tools.description: Create a new storage bucket.

tools.name: scalix_fn_list
tools.description: List all deployed serverless functions.

tools.name: scalix_fn_deploy
tools.description: Deploy a new serverless function from a container image.

tools.name: scalix_fn_invoke
tools.description: Invoke a deployed function with a JSON payload.

tools.name: scalix_kv_get
tools.description: Get a value from the key-value store.

tools.name: scalix_kv_set
tools.description: Set a key-value pair with optional TTL.

tools.name: scalix_kv_list
tools.description: List keys in the key-value store, optionally filtered by prefix.

tools.name: scalix_ai_infer
tools.description: Run AI inference using an LLM. Sends a prompt and returns the model response. Uses the OpenAI-compatible chat completions format.

tools.name: scalix_ai_models
tools.description: List available AI models.

tools.name: scalix_events_publish
tools.description: Publish an event to a topic.

tools.name: scalix_events_topics
tools.description: List all event topics.

tools.name: scalix_cron_create
tools.description: Create a cron schedule to run a function or webhook on a timer.

tools.name: scalix_compute_deploy
tools.description: Deploy a container to the compute platform. Specify an image, replica count, resources, and optional environment variables.

tools.name: scalix_compute_list
tools.description: List all container deployments.

tools.name: scalix_compute_scale
tools.description: Scale a deployment to a specified number of replicas.

tools.name: scalix_compute_delete
tools.description: Delete a container deployment.

tools.name: scalix_sandbox_run
tools.description: Execute code in an isolated sandbox. Supports Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, Go, Rust, and Bash. Returns stdout/stderr.

tools.name: scalix_domain_add
tools.description: Add a custom domain to the project. Returns DNS records to configure.

tools.name: scalix_domain_list
tools.description: List all custom domains and their SSL/verification status.

tools.name: scalix_domain_verify
tools.description: Verify DNS configuration for a custom domain and provision SSL certificate.

tools.name: scalix_build_create
tools.description: Create a new container image build from a Git repository or Dockerfile.

tools.name: scalix_build_status
tools.description: Get the status and logs of a build.

tools.name: scalix_project_create
tools.description: Create a new project within an organization. Returns the project ID and API credentials.

tools.name: scalix_project_list
tools.description: List all projects in an organization.

tools.name: scalix_auth_configure
tools.description: Configure authentication settings for the project — enable/disable signup, MFA, magic links, and set allowed OAuth providers.

tools.name: scalix_status
tools.description: Get the current health status of the Scalix Cloud platform including all service components.

tools.name: scalix_usage
tools.description: Get current usage metrics and costs for a project.

tools.name: scalix_run_deploy
tools.description: Deploy a container service. Specify the image, port, and scaling bounds.

tools.name: scalix_run_list
tools.description: List all deployed container services in the project.

tools.name: scalix_run_scale
tools.description: Scale a running service by adjusting instance bounds.

tools.name: scalix_run_rollback
tools.description: Rollback a service to a previous revision.

tools.name: scalix_search
tools.description: Search the Scalix original-source index — a curated set of original publishers (newsrooms, wires, regulators), not the open web. Returns originals only by default (syndicated copies collapsed to the source that published first), with provenance: source, published date, region, and canonical flag.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "query" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

evidence.snippet: query

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "scalix_compute_deploy" references sensitive artifacts (environment variable). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Deploy a container to the compute platform. Specify an image, replica count, resources, and optional environment variables.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "scalix_project_create" references sensitive artifacts (credentials). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Create a new project within an organization. Returns the project ID and API credentials.

labels.name: scalix_run_rollback
labels.description: Roll a Scalix Run service back to an earlier revision — omit 'revision' to return to the immediately previous one. Traffic switches to the target revision; the replaced revision is kept and can be redeployed later. Use scalix_run_list to see services and their current revisions.

labels.name: scalix_search
labels.description: Search the Scalix original-source index — a curated set of original publishers (newsrooms, wires, regulators), not the open web. Returns originals only by default (syndicated copies collapsed to the source that published first), with provenance: source, published date, region, and canonical flag.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "query" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

evidence.snippet: query

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "scalix_compute_deploy" references sensitive artifacts (environment variable). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Deploy a container to the compute platform. Specify an image, replica count, resources, and optional environment variables.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "scalix_project_create" references sensitive artifacts (credentials). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Create a new project within an organization. Returns the project ID and API credentials.

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "scalix_project_list" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: List every project in an organization, with each project's ID, name, and slug. Use this to resolve a project ID before calling project-scoped tools. Requires ad…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Tool description changed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "query" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "Execute a SQL query against the database. Returns JSON results with column names, rows, and cost breakdown." → after: "Execute a SQL statement against the project's ScalixNova PostgreSQL database. Accepts any single statement (SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, DDL) with optional positional parameters ($1, $2, ...). Returns JSON with column names, rows, row count, and a cost breakdown. Writes are permanent — use create"

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.

findings.title: Tool description changed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "create_sandbox" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "Create an isolated sandbox environment (instant branch) for safe experimentation. Returns a scoped token for the sandbox." → after: "Create an isolated ScalixNova database branch (instant copy-on-write sandbox) for safe experimentation — run queries and migrations against it without touching production data. Returns a scoped token for the sandbox; destroy it with destroy_sandbox or let the TTL expire."

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.

findings.title: Tool description changed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "nl_to_sql" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "Translate a natural language question to SQL using the database schema context." → after: "Translate a plain-language question into a ready-to-run SQL query, using the live database schema (tables, columns, types, relationships) as context. Returns the generated SQL without executing it — review the statement, then run it with the query tool. Useful for exploring unfamiliar schemas; alway"

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.

findings.title: Tool description changed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "scalix_storage_create_bucket" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "Create a new storage bucket." → after: "Create a new Scalix Storage bucket in the project (S3-compatible object storage). Bucket names must be unique within the project. Setting public=true makes every object in the bucket readable without authentication — leave it false (the default) and use presigned URLs for private sharing."

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.

evidence.snippet: before: "Get a value from the key-value store." → after: "Read a single value from the project's key-value store by exact key. Returns the stored value, or a not-found error if the key does not exist or its TTL has expired. Use scalix_kv_list to discover keys by prefix first."

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.

evidence.snippet: before: "List all event topics." → after: "List the event-bus topics in the project, with each topic's ID and name. Use this to find or verify a topic_id before publishing with scalix_events_publish; publishing to a non-existent topic fails."

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.

Irreversible — the deployment and its configuration are not recoverable; recreate with scalix_compute_deploy if needed. Verify the deployment_id with scalix_compute_list first."

evidence.snippet: before: "Deploy a container service. Specify the image, port, and scaling bounds." → after: "Deploy a long-running container service on Scalix Run from a container image. Creates a new service, or a new revision of an existing service with the same name, with health checks, a public HTTPS URL, and autoscaling between min_instances and max_instances (min 0 = scale-to-zero when idle). Billed "

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.

findings.title: Tool description changed since last scan

evidence.snippet: before: "Scale a running service by adjusting instance bounds." → after: "Change the autoscaling bounds of an existing Scalix Run service without redeploying it. Raising min_instances keeps instances warm (no cold starts, higher cost); min_instances 0 enables scale-to-zero when idle. Takes effect immediately. Find service IDs with scalix_run_list."

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.
